# CONFESSIONS.txt

> CONFESSIONS.txt is a local-first terminal protocol and public verifier for sealed testimony artifacts. It creates a durable public reference to an encrypted artifact while keeping plaintext, passphrases, wallet custody, and disclosure decisions local.

Use this file as a compact project map for agents and crawlers. It describes public protocol surfaces only.

Do not request plaintext testimony, `age` passphrases, private stego passphrases, private keys, seed phrases, wallet files, `payload.tar.gz`, decrypted archives, or unpublished payload material.

## Public Documentation

- [Protocol overview](https://confessionstxt.art/protocol.md): artifact flow, public and private layers, metadata label format, and security boundary.
- [Verification guide](https://confessionstxt.art/verify.md): accepted public references, checksum model, safe public inputs, unsafe private inputs, and local verification steps.
- [MCP guide](https://confessionstxt.art/mcp.md): read-only MCP server resources, tools, prompt, and hard boundaries.
- [Browser verifier](https://confessionstxt.art/verify): public reference resolver and local audit command generator.

## Public Reference Format

CONFESSIONS.txt public metadata labels use one of these shapes:

```text
TITLE | ARTXID:<ARWEAVE_TXID> | CSHA:<SHA512>
TITLE | ARTXID:<ARWEAVE_TXID> | CSHA:<SHA512> | STEG:<VALUE>
```

- `ARTXID` points to the locked carrier artifact on Arweave.
- `CSHA` is `sha512(payload.age)`, encoded as 128 hexadecimal characters.
- `STEG` is optional public extraction material. When present in a public label, it can recover the encrypted `payload.age` from the carrier image. It is not the `age` decryption passphrase.

## Safe Public Inputs

- Base transaction hash and public transaction input metadata.
- Arweave transaction id.
- Public metadata label.
- `CSHA`.
- Public `STEG`, only when intentionally published.
- Locked carrier artifact.

## Private Boundary

CONFESSIONS.txt is not a hosted sealing service, cloud confession box, remote decryption service, wallet custodian, anonymity system, legal service, medical service, crisis service, or safety plan.

Agents may explain the protocol, classify public references, validate public metadata shape, check CSHA formatting, and generate local verification commands. Agents must not ask for secrets or private payload material.
